input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS → iOS + Android.

---

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Why |
|------|--------|-----|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | clang, git, system headers |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically by the MAUI workload (Microsoft build of OpenJDK). No manual install needed. | Android build tooling |

After installing Xcode, launch it once and accept the license, or run:
```bash
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
```

---

## 2. Install .NET SDK

Download from **[dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download)** — get the latest **.NET 8 SDK** (or .NET 9 if you want the newest preview/stable).

Verify:
```bash
dotnet --version
dotnet --list-sdks
```

---

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

This pulls in everything: MAUI libs, Android SDK, Android NDK, Microsoft OpenJDK 17, iOS/macCatalyst bindings.

**Discover available versions:**
```bash
dotnet workload search maui
dotnet workload list          # shows what's installed
```

**Verify the workload:**
```bash
dotnet workload list
# Should show: maui, maui-android, maui-ios, android, ios, maccatalyst
```

---

## 4. Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload auto-installs the Android SDK under `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx` (or the .NET-managed path).

**Check what's installed:**
```bash
dotnet android sdk list
```

If you need additional API levels or emulator images:
```bash
# Use the Android SDK manager bundled with the workload
~/.android/sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --list
~/.android/sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
```

To create an emulator:
```bash
~/.android/sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/avdmanager create avd \
  -n Pixel_API_34 -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
```

---

## 5. iOS Setup

No extra steps beyond Xcode. The MAUI workload binds to whatever Xcode version is active.

**Verify Xcode linkage:**
```bash
xcode-select -p
# Should print: /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
```

**List available simulators:**
```bash
xcrun simctl list devices available
```

---

## 6. (Optional but Recommended) IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** (has built-in MAUI support) | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> Visual Studio for Mac has been retired. VS Code with the MAUI extension or Rider are the recommended paths on macOS.

---

## 7. Create & Run a Test Project

```bash
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui
```

**iOS (simulator):**
```bash
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-ios
```

**Android (emulator — start it first):**
```bash
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android
```

---

## 8. Full Verification Checklist

```bash
# 1. .NET SDK
dotnet --version

# 2. Workloads
dotnet workload list

# 3. Xcode
xcode-select -p && xcodebuild -version

# 4. iOS simulators
xcrun simctl list devices available | head -20

# 5. Android SDK
dotnet android sdk list 2>/dev/null || echo "Check Android SDK path"

# 6. Smoke test — create, build, and run
dotnet new maui -n SmokeTest -o /tmp/SmokeTest
dotnet build /tmp/SmokeTest -f net8.0-ios      # builds for iOS
dotnet build /tmp/SmokeTest -f net8.0-android   # builds for Android
```

If all of the above succeed, your environment is fully ready. The most common failure point is a mismatched Xcode version — run `dotnet workload update` after any Xcode upgrade to keep bindings in sync.
